title: Join-Path or string concatenation with unvalidated user/external input for file paths
description: Building file paths by directly concatenating user input or external data without sanitisation enables path traversal attacks (e.g. '../../../etc/passwd' or '..\..\system32'). All file path construction from external input must validate against directory traversal patterns.
detection_method: Manual review of Join-Path calls using variables sourced from user input, Read-Host, or external JSON/XML data without Resolve-Path validation
preventionRule: All file paths constructed from external input must: (1) Use [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath() to resolve, (2) Validate the resolved path starts with the expected base directory, (3) Reject paths containing '..' segments.
remedy: Add path validation: $resolved = [IO.Path]::GetFullPath($userPath); if (-not $resolved.StartsWith($baseDir)) { throw 'Path traversal blocked' }
